name: Dashboard with adversarial real-world data — tests overflow, empty state, and stale data detection

input.frameDescription: Analytics dashboard with: 4 stat cards (Active Users, Revenue, Signups, Churn Rate), a data table (columns: Name, Email, Role, Last Active, Actions) with 5 rows, and a row of user avatars with names beneath them

expectedBehaviors: Clones the frame before injecting any stress data — never mutates the original
Creates separate stress variants: one for long name, one for large number, one for empty table, one for stale date
Screenshots each variant individually to capture the visual result
Flags that 'Wolfeschlegelsteinhausenbergerdorff' overflows the avatar name label and/or table Name column
Flags that '1,234,567,890' may overflow stat card containers or need abbreviation (1.2B)
Flags the empty table (0 rows) needs a designed empty state — not just a blank area
Flags 'March 2019' as stale data — asks if this is intentional or if the design should show a staleness indicator
Reports which specific layers/nodes overflow, not just 'text is long'
Suggests remediation for each issue: truncation with tooltip, number abbreviation, empty state illustration, staleness badge
antiPatterns: Only tests text length — ignores empty state and stale data entirely
Injects all stress data into a single frame instead of creating separate variants
Reports 'text overflows' without identifying which specific component or layer breaks
Mutates the original frame instead of cloning first
Tests the long name but not the large number in stat cards
Treats the empty table as passing because 'no rows' renders without error
Does not screenshot the results — just assumes what will break
Suggests only truncation as a fix for every problem

evaluationCriteria.coverageBreadth: Does it test ALL four stress cases (long name, large number, empty table, stale date), not just text length?
evaluationCriteria.issueSpecificity: Does it identify the exact component/layer that breaks, not just 'overflow detected'?
evaluationCriteria.emptyStateAwareness: Does it flag the empty table as needing a designed empty state with illustration/message, not just 'no data'?
evaluationCriteria.staleDataDetection: Does it recognize 'March 2019' as stale and suggest a staleness indicator or ask if intentional?
evaluationCriteria.nonDestructive: Does it clone the frame before injecting stress data, preserving the original?
evaluationCriteria.remediationQuality: Does it suggest context-appropriate fixes (abbreviation for numbers, truncation+tooltip for names, illustration for empty state)?
